title: ENGINEER
description:
The Engineer – Construction Related Works is responsible for planning, supervising, and executing construction activities in accordance with approved drawings, technical specifications, safety standards, and project schedules. The role ensures quality workmanship, coordination between stakeholders, and timely completion of construction works within budget and regulatory requirements.The Engineer – Installation of Industrial Machinery & Equipment is responsible for planning, supervising, and executing the installation, alignment, commissioning, and handover of industrial machinery and mechanical systems. The role ensures that all mechanical engineering works are completed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with technical specifications, quality standards, and applicable codes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Supervise and coordinate day-to-day construction activities at site.
  • Review and interpret construction drawings, specifications, and method statements.
  • Ensure construction works are executed according to approved plans, standards, and project timelines.
  • Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, consultants, and suppliers.
  • Monitor site progress and prepare daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports.
  • Ensure compliance with quality control procedures and inspection requirements.
  • Implement and enforce health, safety, and environmental (HSE) regulations on site.
  • Identify technical issues and provide effective solutions during construction.
  • Verify materials delivered to site meet project specifications.
  • Support testing, inspections, snagging, and project handover activities.
    Plan, coordinate, and supervise the installation of industrial machinery, production lines, and mechanical equipment.
  • Review mechanical drawings, layouts, specifications, and installation manuals.
  • Ensure proper alignment, leveling, anchoring, and assembly of machinery and equipment.
  • Coordinate with project managers, contractors, vendors, and other engineering disciplines (electrical, civil, automation).
  • Monitor installation progress and ensure compliance with project schedules.
  • Conduct inspections, testing, commissioning, and troubleshooting of installed equipment.
  • Ensure adherence to safety regulations, risk assessments, and method statements.
  • Prepare installation reports, daily progress reports, and technical documentation.
  • Support equipment handover, operator training, and maintenance planning.
  • Identify and resolve site-related technical issues efficiently.
